---
title: "Multiple Testing: FWER and FDR Assignment"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
---

This report documents your implementation of multiple-testing analysis for FWER and FDR control.

Run `uv run python scripts/run_pipeline.py` from the project root. The pipeline must first map `input` to `cleaned` and then map `cleaned` to `output`. The values below are populated from `output/results.json`.

## 2. FWER control

| method | estimated_fwer |
|---|---:|
| uncorrected | 0.9583 |
| bonferroni | 0.0417 |
| holm | 0.0417 |

## 3. FDR and power

| method | estimated_fdr | estimated_power |
|---|---:|---:|
| uncorrected | 0.1768 | 0.6167 |
| benjamini_hochberg | 0.0223 | 0.2889 |
| benjamini_yekutieli | 0.0049 | 0.1100 |

## 4. Brief interpretation

The uncorrected procedure has extremely high family-wise error under the complete null, with estimated FWER of 0.9583. Both Bonferroni and Holm reduce FWER to 0.0417, which is below the target level of 0.05.

For the mixed-null design, the uncorrected rule has the highest power at 0.6167 but also the largest false-discovery rate at 0.1768. Benjamini-Hochberg offers a middle ground, lowering FDR to 0.0223 while keeping more power than Benjamini-Yekutieli, which is the most conservative with FDR 0.0049 and power 0.1100.
